reviews.author: DheerajS
reviews.title: Insignia 55″ Class F50 4K UHD Smart Fire TV — Grea
reviews.body:
I recently upgraded to the Insignia 55″ Class F50 Series LED 4K UHD Smart Fire TV and overall I’m quite pleased. Right out of the box the 4K image looked noticeably sharper and the colors more vibrant compared to my previous 1080p set. The Fire TV interface is intuitive, the Alexa voice remote is handy, and setup was quick.

Watching Netflix, YouTube and streaming sports has been really enjoyable — the screen size and clarity make a difference. On the flip side, when watching late at night in a darker room I did notice that the blacks aren’t as deep as those on premium sets I’ve seen, and fast-motion content (especially high-end gaming) lacks the ultra-smoothness of higher refresh-rate TVs. The built-in speakers are good for general viewing, but if you want full cinematic audio you’ll want a soundbar.

Bottom line: For the price this is a great value 55″ 4K Smart TV. If you don’t demand the very top-tier picture specs, it hits a sweet spot of size, functionality, and affordability.

Would I recommend it? Yes — if you’re after a solid upgrade and value smart features and screen size more than ultra-premium specs.

reviews.author: KierstonY
reviews.title: Fantastic TV for the Price!
reviews.body:
I recently purchased the Insignia 55-inch Amazon Fire TV, and I couldn’t be happier! The picture quality is crisp and vibrant, with colors that really pop, and the 4K resolution makes movies and shows look stunning. The built-in Amazon Fire TV interface is seamless, making it super easy to navigate apps like Netflix, Prime Video, Hulu, and more—all without needing an extra streaming device.

The remote is intuitive and responsive, and I love that it has Alexa voice control—it makes searching for shows or controlling smart home devices a breeze. Setup was quick and straightforward, and the TV feels solid and well-built for the price.

Overall, this is an excellent choice if you’re looking for a large, smart TV that delivers amazing value. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: LostKid13
reviews.title: Great standalone Roku tv on a budget
reviews.body:
I guess this is a new budget model but it seems like the only difference is the remote that’s included! So no voice remote (so has to be line of sight) but all others functionality remains the same as the other 55” base model as far as I can tell. Typical easy roku setup experience. Easy to customize the Home Screen to minimize distractions (was setting up for a family member) and love the fact most streaming accounts synced across devices automatically. Connected to soundbar via HDMI ARC and had immediate volume control via the included Roku remote. Incredibly light weight so very easy to wall mount. 

Built in speakers don’t seem to have the specialized wall mount setting but was setting up a soundbar anyway. Display is plenty bright enough for a room with a window. 

Smart features are quick and responsive especially when compared with the Xfinity cable box they were using prior.

reviews.author: LexiH
reviews.title: Incredible Value – The Smartest TV I’ve Owned!
reviews.body:
I couldn’t be happier with this purchase. Right out of the box, the setup was incredibly fast and intuitive—the Roku interface remains the gold standard for simplicity.

The 4K HDR picture quality is stunningly clear, making movies and sports look vibrant and true-to-life. I was particularly impressed with the Roku Smart Picture feature, which automatically optimizes the settings so I don’t have to fiddle with menus to get the best look. The audio is also surprisingly crisp for a flat screen, and the auto speech clarity makes a huge difference during dialogue-heavy scenes.

One of my favorite features is the voice remote; it makes searching across different streaming apps a breeze. Plus, the lost remote finder has already saved me multiple times! Whether you’re a heavy streamer or a casual viewer, this TV offers a premium experience at a fraction of the cost of higher-end brands. Highly recommended!

reviews.title: Great price and quality

Picked this up for about 250 USD and I have to say it is a nice upgrade. The UI is easy to use and added plus is when you plug in a new device to an HDMI connection on the TV, it immediately identifies what the device is (i.e., Nintendo Switch, PS5, etc.). This was well worth the money and then some. I don't feel the need to splurge on a expensive television at the moment. If you're looking for something with decent quality at a budget, this TV is perfect.

The slim MetalStream design looks sleek and modern—it has a premium metal back and thin bezels that blend right into my wall. Setup was a breeze: plug it in, connect to Wi-Fi, and you're good to go in minutes.

Excellent Value QLED TV – Bright, Fast, and Easy to Use

For the price, the picture quality really impressed me—colors are vibrant, brightness is strong, and the QLED panel makes a noticeable difference compared to standard LED TVs. 4K content looks sharp and detailed, and even regular HD content upscales nicely.
The Fire TV interface is fast and easy to navigate, with quick access to all my streaming apps like Netflix, Prime Video, YouTube, and Hulu. Alexa voice control works great and makes searching and controlling the TV simple. Setup was quick and straightforward right out of the box.
Sound quality is better than expected for a flat TV, and the overall build feels solid. This TV is perfect for movies, sports, and gaming, especially if you’re looking for a high-quality 55” QLED without paying premium-brand prices.
Highly recommend this TV for anyone wanting excellent performance and smart features at a great value.

title: Hisense - 55" Class S7 Series CanvasTV 4K QLED TV with Hi-Matte Display and UltraSlim Wall Mount (2024)
ai_summary: Customers enjoy the 55" Class S7 Series CanvasTV for its excellent picture quality and the super slim wall mount that keeps it flush against the wall. The TV also excels at glare reduction, making it easy to view in various lighting conditions. Many appreciate the art mode and overall appearance, noting that it resembles a painting, and find the installation process to be straightforward.

reviews.author: GbemisolaA
reviews.title: Art in a TV!!!!
reviews.body:
This Hisense Canvas TV has completely changed how my space feels. It’s not just a television, it’s a statement piece. When it’s mounted on the wall, it genuinely looks like art, the kind that makes people pause, look twice, and ask questions. Guests are always intrigued, and the moment I reveal it’s actually a TV, the reaction is pure amazement.
The design is sleek and refined, blending effortlessly into my décor. Instead of a black screen dominating the room, I now have a living canvas that adds character and creativity to my wall. The artwork display is stunning, with rich colors and fine detail that truly mimics a framed piece.
When it is time to watch something, the quality is just as impressive. The picture is crisp, vibrant, and immersive, with excellent contrast and clarity. It delivers both beauty and performance without compromise.
What I love most is how the Hisense Canvas TV elevates the entire room. It feels intentional, artistic, and modern, perfect for anyone who values design as much as technology. This TV doesn’t just entertain; it inspires.

reviews.author: JonM
reviews.title: Beautiful Picture, Beautiful Art
reviews.body:
The Canvas Art TV 55" has excellent picture quality, with sharp details and rich color that make movies and shows look fantastic. The screen does an impressive job reducing reflections and glare from lamps and windows, so it is much easier on the eyes in a bright room.

The multiple brightness and picture mode controls make the TV highly customizable, so it is simple to adjust the image to suit different times of day or types of content. The built-in gallery of artwork is especially pleasing and truly transforms the screen into a digital painting when you are not watching TV.

Overall, this TV feels like both a high-quality display and a piece of decor, adding a lovely, sophisticated touch to any room in the house.

reviews.author: BenjaminF
reviews.title: Perfect addition for the yacht.
reviews.body: I installed this where a piece of art used to hang in the dining area on a yacht. It was replaced with a TV that always looked out of place. Now we have the best of both worlds. It really does blend in and add a bit of class back to the space. As a TV it is great as well. I would recommend a sound bar or sound system. the speakers are not amazing.

reviews.author: p8r8d1gm
reviews.title: Excellent price and quality for a picture frame TV
reviews.body: First, we got this type of television because we wanted to hang it in our living room and it substitute for a piece of art if we weren’t watching TV on it… It has fulfilled that purpose amazingly.  I have not owned the TVs long enough to do a longevity review, but first impressions: great price versus other picture frame TVs, excellent picture quality, excellent sound… These were cheaper than the TCL‘s, far cheaper than the Samsung and I can’t really say that there’s much quality difference (could be my unsophisticated eyes). This is just my experience so far… But we’ve had one of them for over three months and it’s been fantastic!

reviews.author: Zulu
reviews.title: Artwork mode fooled the AI!!!
reviews.body:
Hisense Canvas TV 55” (2024) is a great option for those of us looking for a frame type tv. The artwork mode is so good that when I looked for a pic in my phone’s photo library using the search field prompt “painting” the 2nd upload is what came up! Type in television and the first uploaded photo came up. The magnetic frame aesthetic in teak with the included flush wall mount help complete the illusion that you’re looking at artwork instead of a TV.  The screen’s matte finish goes great with the free digital artwork Hisense furnishes with the purchase of your TV. NO SUBSCRIPTION FEES unlike the Samsung Frame TVs requires for their artwork access.

The instillation went smoothly and if you have experience hanging TVs this should be no sweat for you. One thing to keep in mind is that the power cable and any subsequent cable connections like HDMI for instance will need to be recessed to maintain the flushed mount. This is important if you want the ability to toggle between flatscreen TV and a painting hanging on the wall. If the TV isn’t flush against the wall then you might as well save some money and get a normal flat screen.

I went from an 85” Samsung TV that’s about 6 years old to the Hisense Canvas 55”. Bigger isn’t always better and the 55” fits nicely over the fireplace. We live in southern Florida and won’t be using the fireplace for anything other than candles unless something crazy happens so the TV won’t be exposed to excessive heat.

All in all, I’m loving the Canvas TV! We have gotten many compliments on the TV and on the paintings while in artwork mode. The Google powered interface is easy to use and very intuitive. It also pairs easily with Amazon Echo for voice commands when you’re away from the remote control. The picture is vibrant and beautiful when I’m looking at a Van Gogh or just watching Netflix. Finally the Hisense Canvas is less expensive than the Samsung Frame and doesn’t have that bulking connector box to deal with. 

PS: I bought from Best Buy, delivered, and installed the Canvas tv 100% by myself.

reviews.author: TVCritic
reviews.title: Mostly good experience
reviews.body:
So far the TV is good.  The pros are great picture and the Matt screen with artwork on it is great to look at!  The super thin design and included mounting bracket is what sold me and they work great.
The cons are: the dimensions advertised are at best misleading and at worst outright wrong, which creates big issues in the remodel I was doing for a neighbor with this TV as a built in.
The remote setup and menus in general feel awkward and somewhat unlike other TVs I have set up.
Sound is bad, but I wasn’t expecting good sound so I don’t take away rating for that.

reviews.author: NathanT
reviews.title: Exactly what I was looking for. Very Cool!
reviews.body: I have been waiting since the day this product was announced to purchase it as I have been thinking about getting Samsungs competing product, but hate the fact you have to subscribe to get more art and the fact it was pretty spendy for what you get.  I have had Hisense before and am a fan so the very first day the 55 went on sale I purchased it.  It was delivered a few days later and I got it on the wall.  The 55 was the biggest I could fit flush against my wall and we are downsizing from a 65 so I was a little worried it would look too small.  I am pleased with the size and even more please at the anti glare screen.  We have huge windows behind the tv and have been fighting glare for years.  As you can see in the pictures, glare is non existant, if it wasnt as cool looking as it is, the anti glare is worth it on its own.  The art is stunning and the google tv is a good smart system, I havent used it yet on this tv as I just got it hung, but I have experience with it and like it.  There are some drawbacks on the installation.  All of the cables are routed out of the bottom of the tv, why would they design this so the cables could be visible when that defeats the purpose of it looking like art?  There is a small indentation in the back of the tv deep enough for the cables to fit without pushing the tv forward, I have a recessed box for my power and HDMI cable, but I had to lift the tv so that some of that recessed part of the tv overlapped the recessed box in my wall.   this cause me to have to raise the tv a bit more than I wanted.  If you are planning to have the cables hidden, it is imperative you have a recessed are for power and connections or you can kiss the flush mounting good bye.  Even with the recessed, trying to get the cables to sit right took me and my daughter with small hands to get behind there and route the cables.  As you can see in the photos, it is perfectly flush.  You can set the arework to rotate every 5, 10, 60 min etc or sit on the same one until you choose he next one.  You can tell it how long to wait before going to sleep if no one is in the room, you can set do not disturb times where it will not come on if someone is detected.  Its a cool product and I am glad I waited for the Hisense.

reviews.title: I Regret Making This My Living Room TV
reviews.body: We’ve had this unit a little over a year. It works well and the picture is ok, though it suffers shadows in very dark scenes. We wall-mounted ours and the rear channels are very shallow and not large enough for the cables if you’re using all the inputs - and I even used low-profile and thin versions when available. The internal graphics processor is “meh” and the main processor is glitchy. We often have to hard close apps because they’re not working correctly. We’ve also had issues with log-in/password management and a few apps require log-in at every open. Overall, it’s a nice secondary/bedroom TV but there are better primary TV options. We will probably replace this as our living room unit in another few months and put this in the guest room.

title: TCL - 55" Class QM5K Series 4K UHD HDR QD-Mini LED Smart TV with Google TV (2025)
ai_summary: Customers frequently mention the excellent picture quality and brightness of the 55" Class QM5K Series 4K UHD HDR QD-Mini LED Smart TV. Users also appreciate the easy setup and the performance of the Google TV software. However, some customers have noted concerns about the connectivity options and the viewing angle.

reviews.author: SIMON
reviews.title: Top Budget TV for Xbox Series S
reviews.body:
This TV is easily had under $400 with quite the feature set, it shares a panel with the QM6 but this QM5 has a lesser motherboard so it tops out at 4k 60hrz, part of why it's perfect for the Series S, it can also downscale to 1440p for 144hrz. The picture Quality on any setting is good enough out of the box but if it's not to your liking there are many quite granular pictures settings to get the picture that suits you best. Some settings like IMAX or Dolby Vision Gaming doesn't let you change some settings though (not certain but I think it depends on what HDR the source is using) the TV will display in the top left corner what HDR is being used for a brief moment. The Sound is also quite surprising, very good and rich sound coming from the Onkyo 2.1 system, note that only the 55in and above will have that Onkyo system the 50in is pretty basic in that regard, in my opinion if you need the TV itself to have good sound it would be worthwhile going for the 55in and above. The legs that come in the box aren't confidence inspiring but I haven't had any issues with them. It is a Google Smart TV which means you'll need some kind of Google account if you want to use that side of the TV, thankfully though if you don't, it does allow for setting it up as a basic TV which is something more smart tvs should do. It does have an app to use your phone as a remote, and the remote that comes in the box is very nice, it has a motion activated backlight which can be turned off and it is connected through Bluetooth so the remote doesn't need line of sight to the TV to work it. Assuming the 55in the best place for a TV like this would be between a living room, spare room, or bedroom TV, I have this in my bedroom and my room is quite small so it takes up a lot of space but if the room you have in mind for it is a bit on the larger side it will fit perfectly. 
This TCL QM5K is a standout in the budget category for being a 4k QD-Mini LED panel with Full Arry Local dimming with 300 zones, all the HDR standards are supported, a gaming mode that supports downscaling resolution for higher refresh rate (peak is 144hz) and a Auto Low Latency Mode (ALLM) good sound from Onkyo with the rear subwoofers (only available on the 55in+) this TV is quite the package coming in under $400USD

reviews.author: BlownCamaro
reviews.title: Most affordable mini-LED on the market today!
reviews.body:
If you don't need 4k120hz (and you probably don't!) then this is a great tv for the money. Gaming on it is fantastic. It's bright enough, has excellent speakers, motion is decent and powers on quickly. The Google OS is the best that there is. 

Positives:

- It can do 1440p120hz which is the sweet spot for PC gaming and console gaming! Much better than 4k60hz that competing sets offer (which it can also do but it not limited to). Why? Because although PS5 and Series X can do 4k120hz in LIMITED games, they always downscale the graphics rendering to near 1440p, then upscale it to 4k anyway! So why you may think you are getting 4k120hz, you aren't. The hardware just isn't capable of it except MAYBE the PS5 Pro.

- You can set it to boot up on last input used which means it can power up and be ready on that input in around 5 seconds. This is blistering quick and the best I have seen on any tv. It's just like a monitor in this respect.

- The remote is decent, but not nearly as nice as what came with my QM7 or the QM7K (same only different color). It gets the job done though.
Streaming apps work great. No problems there at all.
Menus are really good and quite detailed on the settings available

- It has the EXACT SAME panel as the QK6K which costs $150 more. Same brightness, same contrast, same colors. The difference is the QM6K has a better HDMI board with 4k144hz on 2 inputs and 1 4k120hz with 4 inputs total. If you don't need that feature (I didn't), then save the money.

- You get an extra free 90 days of warranty through TCL when you register your unit so don't forget to do that! So that's now a 15-month warranty versus other brands 12-month.


Negatives:

- Not bright enough for great HDR. Yeah, it can do it but you won't see spots like you will with the TCL 7 or 8 series. But you know what? I have the brightness turned down to ZERO on my QM7 because it hurts my eyes in my bedroom. Seriously, too bright. This tv is just about right for normal tv viewing.

- Really poor off angle viewing if you sit close to it. I tried to use it as a monitor, and it does not work for that. You need to be a minimum of 6 feet away and 8 feet looks even better. The way this mini-LED works aims the brightness straight ahead and very little to the sides. You'll get washed out images AND color change - usually reddish tint. So straight on and far enough back it's great. It's not a plasma or an OLED with perfect viewing angles!

- The VESA mounts are very low on the tv. This could be a problem with some tv stands and was for mine. I had to drill holes 4 inches lower because the tv was way too high. If you have a wall mount, keep this in mind - you might be modifying it. It comes with the cheapest "chicken feet" possible and I would never use them.
Only 3 HDMI inputs, one of which is eARC. Might be an issue for some, but not I.

In summary, I play games in SDR on this and it is so bright I have to turn the brightness down to 75 or it hurts my eyes. Some reviewers said this tv is dim - yeah compared to a QM8K it is! But it's WAY brighter than anything made 10 years ago. If I had to guess, it looks to be about 400nits in SDR and 500nits+ in HDR.

For the price, YOU WILL NOT find a better tv - period. I spent months comparing brands and this QM5K has no competition at this time in this price range.

title: TCL - 55" Class F35-Series 4K UHD HDR LED Smart Fire TV (2025)
ai_summary: Customers admire the 55" Class F35-Series 4K UHD HDR LED Smart Fire TV (2025) for its excellent picture quality, vibrant colors, ease of use, and affordable price. Many find it to be a great value and appreciate its lightweight design. However, some customers have expressed concerns regarding the remote control, slow speed, app performance, and occasional freezing of the operating system.

reviews.author: Aman
reviews.title: Surprisingly Good TV for the Price
reviews.body:
I wasn’t expecting much when I bought this TV, but it honestly surprised me in a good way. The picture is sharp and clear, and 4K content looks really nice, especially for movies and regular TV streaming. Colors look natural and not washed out, and it handles HDR better than I thought it would.

The Fire TV system is easy to use and quick to set up. I like that everything is in one place and I don’t have to switch between different devices. The remote is simple, and using Alexa to search for shows actually comes in handy more than I expected.

Sound is fine for everyday watching. The TV itself looks clean and modern, and the thin edges make the screen feel bigger.

Overall, this feels like a solid, dependable TV that does what it’s supposed to do without issues. For the price, I think it’s a really good deal and I’d buy it again.

The built-in Fire TV is incredibly convenient. I love having all my streaming apps in one place, and the interface is easy to navigate. Setting it up was a breeze, and I was streaming my favorite content within minutes.
For the price, this TV is an absolute steal. The picture quality rivals more expensive models, and the smart features are top-notch. If you're looking for a budget-friendly 4K TV that doesn't compromise on performance, the TCL F35-Series is an excellent choice.

reviews.author: LBTanner
reviews.title: Excellent Picture and Res (Add speakers!)
reviews.body:
I decided on this TV after user reviews warned: TCL TVs last years or weeks, no in between. For $233, including the 2-yr protection in case I got a 2-wk lemon, this is one of the best valued TVs on the market. Even through the holiday sales, the price was the most competitive. 

The picture is extremely clear even when viewing the picture from the sides (see images). Connecting to user accounts and creating admins was also a breeze. iOS, Win10, Win11, and ChromiumOS had no issue connecting; neither did the game consoles.

This TV is also ideal for folks who live upstairs or alone. It is ridiculously light to carry even for short petite people (I'm 8 in. taller than it).

The only quality-check so far on the TCL 55" is the sound. The audio is C-/C compared to the rest of the product. I got a $27 Insignia sound bar from the Best Buy outlet that has made a world of difference on the sound.

Everything else about the product is 5 out of 5. Excellent value for an excellent product.

reviews.body:
I recently purchased the TCL 55" Class F35-Series 4K UHD HDR LED Smart Fire TV, and I have to say I'm pretty impressed. The picture quality is amazing for the price, the 4K resolution is clear and vibrant. The HDR really enhances the colors, making everything look more dynamic. Setup was pretty straightforward too, especially with the Fire TV OS built in – I had all my streaming apps up and running within minutes. The remote is simple to use, with voice control that actually works better than I expected.

However, there are a few small downsides. First, the sound quality isn’t the best. I had to connect a soundbar to get a fuller, richer audio experience. Also, the TV’s interface can sometimes feel a bit sluggish when switching between apps or scrolling through menus. Nothing major, but it's noticeable when you're used to a faster system. 
Overall, this TCL TV offers excellent value for the price and delivers a solid 4K experience. Highly recommend it for anyone on a budget but still wanting a quality smart TV.
